questions.answer: A speech is a formal, verbal communication delivered to an audience, usually on a specific topic.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of a toastmaster?
questions.answer: The primary purpose of a toastmaster is to introduce speakers, manage the proceedings, and ensure a smooth flow of the event.
An extempore speech is an impromptu speech delivered without prior preparation or rehearsal.

questions.q: List the steps to be followed before delivering a speech.
questions.answer: The steps to be followed before delivering a speech are: (i) Research and preparation (ii) Writing the speech (iii) Rehearsal (iv) Arrangement of the physical environment.

questions.q: What are the qualities of a good speaker?
questions.answer: The qualities of a good speaker are: (i) Confidence (ii) Clarity of thought and expression (iii) Good voice and diction (iv) Eye contact and body language.
An after-dinner speech is a speech delivered at the end of a meal, usually to entertain or inform the audience.

questions.q: What are the functions of a toastmaster?
questions.answer: The functions of a toastmaster are: (i) Introduction of speakers (ii) Management of proceedings (iii) Maintenance of order and decorum (iv) Ensuring a smooth flow of the event.

questions.q: What are the advantages of an impromptu speech?
questions.answer: The advantages of an impromptu speech are: (i) Flexibility (ii) Spontaneity (iii) Authenticity (iv) Less time-consuming.

questions.q: List the steps to be followed while delivering a speech.
questions.answer: The steps to be followed while delivering a speech are: (i) Beginning with a hook (ii) Making eye contact with the audience (iii) Varying the tone and pitch (iv) Using gestures and body language.
An after-luncheon speech is a speech delivered at the end of a meal, usually to entertain or inform the audience.

questions.q: What are the characteristics of a good speech?
questions.answer: The characteristics of a good speech are: (i) Clarity of thought and expression (ii) Good voice and diction (iii) Proper use of gestures and body language (iv) Effective use of visual aids (v) Engagement with the audience.
key_points: Proper use of gestures and body language

questions.q: What are the functions of a master of ceremonies?
questions.answer: The functions of a master of ceremonies are: (i) Introduction of speakers (ii) Management of proceedings (iii) Maintenance of order and decorum (iv) Ensuring a smooth flow of the event (v) Coordinating with the speakers and other participants.
key_points: Coordination with speakers and other participants

questions.q: What is the role of a speech writer?
questions.answer: The role of a speech writer is to: (i) Research and gather information on the topic (ii) Organize the ideas and structure the speech (iii) Write the speech in a clear and concise manner (iv) Ensure that the speech is engaging and persuasive (v) Edit and proofread the speech.

questions.q: What are the qualities of a good speech writer?
questions.answer: The qualities of a good speech writer are: (i) Research skills (ii) Writing skills (iii) Understanding of the audience and topic (iv) Creativity and originality (v) Ability to engage and persuade the audience.
questions.topic: Qualities of a Good Speech Writer

questions.q: What are the steps to be followed while preparing a speech?
questions.answer: The steps to be followed while preparing a speech are: (i) Research and information gathering (ii) Organization of ideas and structure (iii) Writing the speech (iv) Rehearsal and practice (v) Editing and proofreading.
